# accepts

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Higher level content negotiation for Node.js. Extracted from [koa](https://github.com/koajs/koa) for general use.

In addition to negotiating content-types, it allows:

- Negotiation of charset
- Negotiation of encoding
- Negotiation of language

It effectively serves as a more user-friendly API for the [negotiator](https://www.npmjs.com/package/negotiator) package, which is used internally.

## Installation

```bash
$ npm install accepts
```

## API

```js
var accepts = require('accepts')
```

### accepts(req)

Create a new `Accepts` object for the given `req` (Node.js HTTP request object).

#### .charset(charsets)

Return the first accepted charset. If nothing in `charsets` is accepted, then `false` is returned.

```js
// Accept: utf-8, iso-8859-1;q=0.2, utf-7;q=0.5
accepts(req).charset(['utf-7', 'utf-8']);
// => 'utf-8'
```

#### .charsets()

Return the charsets that the client accepts, in order of preference.

```js
// Accept-Charset: utf-8, iso-8859-1;q=0.2, utf-7;q=0.5
accepts(req).charsets();
// => ['utf-8', 'utf-7', 'iso-8859-1']
```

#### .encoding(encodings)

Return the first accepted encoding. If nothing in `encodings` is accepted, then `false` is returned.

```js
// Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
accepts(req).encoding(['gzip', 'deflate']);
// => 'gzip'
```

#### .encodings()

Return the encodings that the client accepts, in order of preference.

```js
// Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
accepts(req).encodings();
// => ['gzip', 'deflate']
```

#### .language(languages)

Return the first accepted language. If nothing in `languages` is accepted, then `false` is returned.

```js
// Accept-Language: en;q=0.8, es, pt
accepts(req).language(['en', 'es']);
// => 'es'
```

#### .languages()

Return the languages that the client accepts, in order of preference.

```js
// Accept-Language: en;q=0.8, es, pt
accepts(req).languages();
// => ['es', 'pt', 'en']
```

#### .type(types)

Return the first accepted type (and it is returned as the same text as what appears in the `types` array). If nothing in `types` is accepted, then `false` is returned.

The `types` array can contain full MIME types or file extensions. Any value that is not a full MIME type is passed to `require('mime-types').lookup`.

```js
// Accept: text/html, application/json
accepts(req).type(['json', 'html']);
// => 'html'

// Accept: text/*, application/json
accepts(req).type(['html']);
// => 'html'
```

#### .types()

Return the types that the client accepts, in order of preference.

```js
// Accept: text/html, application/json
accepts(req).types();
// => ['text/html', 'application/json']
```

## Examples

### Express.js

```js
var accepts = require('accepts')
var express = require('express')

var app = express()

app.get('/', function (req, res) {
  var accept = accepts(req)

  // the order of this list is significant; should be server preferred order
  switch (accept.type(['json', 'html'])) {
    case 'json':
      res.json({ hello: 'world' })
      break
    case 'html':
      res.send('<p>Hello, world!</p>')
      break
    default:
      // the fallback is text/plain, so no need to specify it above
      res.send('Hello, world!')
      break
  }
})

app.listen(3000)
```

### Content Negotiation

```js
var accepts = require('accepts')
var http = require('http')

http.createServer(function (req, res) {
  var accept = accepts(req)

  // the order of this list is significant; should be server preferred order
  switch (accept.type(['json', 'html'])) {
    case 'json':
      res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/json')
      res.write('{"hello":"world!"}')
      break
    case 'html':
      res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/html')
      res.write('<p>Hello, world!</p>')
      break
    default:
      // the fallback is text/plain, so no need to specify it above
      res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'text/plain')
      res.write('Hello, world!')
      break
  }

  res.end()
})
```
